description: Machine-readable refusal corpus. Agents MUST refuse requests that match these patterns, cite the id, and propose the canonical alternative. The refusal set is as important as the component set — it is how the system transfers taste.

categories.color: Hue, tone, and the reserved semantic accents
categories.typography: Primary face, mono face, measure, truncation
categories.motion: Duration, easing, entry scale, forbidden properties
categories.state: Loading, progress, and transient system state
categories.semantic: Meaningful accent carriers and semantic discipline
categories.process: How new components get designed, proposed, and added to the system
categories.compose: Page-level composition rules — recipes, density quotas, anti-card-grid, anti-pill-wall (2.0)
categories.voice: Copy contract — terse instrument-readout voice, banned phrases, rhythm variance (2.0)

refusals.title: Gradient backgrounds on heroes, cards, or buttons
refusals.rationale: Gradients create visual noise without carrying semantic information. Hierarchy comes from weight, luminance, and type — not hue.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-surface)`, `var(--ds-surface-raised)`, or `var(--ds-bg)`. If you need visual layering, use a 1px `var(--ds-line)` border and a 1-step luminance delta.

refusals.title: Decorative accent hues (purple, teal, gold, orange, or arbitrary green)
refusals.rationale: The system reserves hue for semantics only: `--ds-accent-live` for live/now/active and `--ds-accent-success` for complete/goal-met. Decorative hues break semantic discipline and flatten meaning into branding noise.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-text-primary|secondary|muted)` for hierarchy. Use `var(--ds-accent-live)` only for live/now/active or `var(--ds-accent-success)` only for terminal completion, and only in sanctioned carriers (see R-SEMANTIC-001).

refusals.title: Semantic accent on a CTA button
refusals.rationale: Semantic accents encode status meaning. A primary CTA is directional, not temporal or terminal. Coloring it with either accent confuses what the hue means across the interface.
refusals.correct: Primary button uses `var(--ds-text-primary)` fill on dark bg with `var(--ds-bg)` ink — a reversed-contrast chip. Accents stay reserved for live/status or terminal completion semantics.

refusals.title: Inter, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, Open Sans, Lato, Poppins, Nunito, Space Grotesk, or `system-ui` as primary face
refusals.rationale: These faces are the recognizable 'AI slop' type signature. Using them defeats the premise of the system. Sous ships with Geist + Geist Mono for a reason: precision, dense info, tabular data.
refusals.correct: Primary: `var(--ds-font-sans)` (Geist). Mono: `var(--ds-font-mono)` (Geist Mono). Brand overrides belong in an override layer, never the default.

refusals.title: Full-width body paragraph
refusals.rationale: Body text past 65ch is hard to read. Eye has to jump too far on line wrap.
refusals.correct: Cap body at `max-width: var(--ds-measure)` (65ch).

refusals.title: Three-dot ellipsis literal (`...`) instead of `…`
refusals.rationale: Three dots versus the ellipsis character typeset differently. Using the character is a small taste marker.
refusals.correct: Use `…` (U+2026) in any user-facing truncation.

refusals.title: Shadow blur ≥ 25px
refusals.rationale: Soft large-radius shadows are the AI-slop elevation signature. Dark UI should separate surfaces with 1px borders and a luminance delta, not shadow theater.
refusals.correct: Dark cards: 1px `var(--ds-line)` border, no shadow. Floating menus/toasts: `var(--ds-elev-1)`. Modals: `var(--ds-elev-2)`. Anything larger is out.

refusals.title: Dark card with radius > 16px (lg)
refusals.rationale: Large radii on dark cards read as soft / marketing / blob. The system is precision-leaning.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-radius-md)` (12px) for cards. `lg` (16px) is the ceiling.

refusals.title: `transition: all`
refusals.rationale: `all` is opaque to intent. It animates properties you didn't mean to, creates layout thrash, and hides perf issues.
refusals.correct: Enumerate: `transition: opacity var(--ds-dur-micro) var(--ds-ease-out), transform var(--ds-dur-standard) var(--ds-ease-out);`

refusals.title: Duration > 300ms
refusals.rationale: Sous motion ceiling is 300ms. Past that, the UI feels slow and agentic-y.
refusals.correct: Use `var(--ds-dur-micro)` (140ms), `var(--ds-dur-standard)` (220ms), or `var(--ds-dur-deliberate)` (280ms). 300ms is the hard ceiling.

refusals.title: Entry animation from `scale(0)`
refusals.rationale: scale(0) entries feel cartoony and draw attention to the animation itself. Real UI enters from near its final size.
refusals.correct: Start from `scale(0.95)` minimum. Exit can go lower, but entries never.

refusals.title: `color` transition on hover
refusals.rationale: Color transitions on hover read as lazy and slow. Use opacity or background-color changes.
refusals.correct: For hover affordance, change `background-color` or `opacity`, not `color`.

refusals.title: Spacing literal off the 8pt scale
refusals.rationale: The system snaps to 8pt increments (plus 2/4 for micro). Values like 15, 18, 20, 26 are the off-grid signature of hand-placed UI.

refusals.title: Interactive target below 44×44 (without invisible hit padding)
refusals.rationale: Touch hit areas under 44×44 fail WCAG 2.5.5. Visual size can be smaller — hit area cannot.
refusals.correct: Visible size as designed; hit area enforced via `min-height/min-width: var(--ds-size-interactive-min)` or `width: max(100%, var(--ds-size-interactive-min))` pattern.

refusals.title: Semantic accent used outside sanctioned carriers
refusals.rationale: Accent tokens are semantic carriers, not theme colors. `accent-live` is for live/now/active. `accent-success` is for terminal completion. Painting them onto arbitrary surfaces breaks the most important meaning contract in the system.
refusals.correct: Carry `accent-live` via `<LiveDot>`, `<InlineStatus tone="live">`, `<Pill live>`, `<Toast tone="live">`, an anomaly point in `<DottedChart>`, a running column in `<DotTimeline>`, or the head of `<PulseTrail>`. (`<LiveBlock>` is also a live indicator, but its chasing motion carries the signal — it does not paint in `accent-live`.) Carry `accent-success` via `<SegmentedBar>` at full completion, a documented success endpoint in `<DottedChart>`, a done column in `<DotTimeline>`, a done trail dot in `<PulseTrail>`, or a cleared line in `<TetrisLoader>`. If a new semantic genuinely needs an accent, the PR must update DESIGN.md 'Accent discipline' and this refusal entry.

refusals.title: Two accent meanings compete inside one cluster
refusals.rationale: If live and success fight for attention inside one cluster, users stop trusting hue as information. Accent must read as meaning, not as decoration.
refusals.correct: Pick the dominant semantic for the cluster. Live and success may coexist only when they describe different stages of the same workflow or live in clearly separate clusters.

refusals.title: Skeleton or shimmer loading placeholders
refusals.rationale: Skeletons are generic product-ui filler. In this system, loading state should read as an instrument update, not a blurred promise of future layout.
refusals.correct: Use terse mono status text for indefinite work or `SegmentedBar` when total progress is known.

refusals.title: Glass morphism (blur + translucent background)
refusals.rationale: The 2023–2025 AI-slop signature. Over-used in generated marketing pages. Not wrong in every context, but a red flag in a dense instrument system.
refusals.correct: Use solid surfaces. If overlay context genuinely requires backdrop treatment, document the exception.

refusals.title: Emoji icons in instrument UI
refusals.rationale: Emoji in dashboard/data/agent UI is the 'AI-assistant personality theater' signature. Instrument UIs use geometric icons or text.
refusals.correct: Use simple glyph text (● ▲ ■ → ↗), monospace labels, or SVG icons matching stroke and size tokens.

refusals.title: 'Sparkle' or 'magic wand' affordances for AI features
refusals.rationale: Sparkles are the 2023 AI-slop convention. Sous treats AI as an instrument, not magic.
refusals.correct: Name the action (`Summarize`, `Regenerate`, `Cite`). Use geometric or text-only affordance. Reserve the accent-live dot for active processing, not for 'AI present'.

refusals.title: Display or h1 falls back to a serif when Cash Sans is unavailable
refusals.rationale: When Cash Sans isn't licensed/loaded, models reach for a serif fallback because hero headlines 'feel editorial.' That breaks the system's voice — sous-ds is a monospace-instrument aesthetic, never a serif page. The 1.0 case-study page rendered the hero in serif because no fallback was named.
refusals.correct: Display and h1 fallback when Cash Sans is unavailable: `var(--ds-font-mono)` (Geist Mono) at the same display size with `font-weight: 600`. Never a serif. Set the `font-family` stack as `"Cash Sans", var(--ds-font-mono)` so the cascade reaches mono, not the system serif.

refusals.title: Sequenced or DAG content rendered as a flat card grid
refusals.rationale: Pipeline pages, milestone strips, multi-stage processes, and DAG-shaped content rendered as a grid of `<Card>` elements lose their primary information: the order, flow, and per-stage state. The 1.0 case-study page rendered a 10-stage pipeline as a 5-card flat grid — the biggest creative miss on that page.
refusals.correct: Use a structured primitive: `<DotTimeline>` for time-ordered stages with state, `<PulseTrail>` for live-now activity, `<DottedChart>` for per-stage density. See `docs/specs/sous-ds-v2-composition-recipes.md` (PipelineMap, MilestoneStrip, AgentLog).
appliesTo: any page describing a pipeline, workflow, sequence, DAG, or multi-stage process
any 'show me how X flows from Y to Z' request

refusals.title: Same composition recipe used more than twice on one page
refusals.rationale: Card monotony — every section the same `eyebrow + h1 + body + grid-of-cards` rhythm — is the dominant 1.0 failure mode. Repeating any single recipe more than twice flattens hierarchy and removes scannability.
refusals.correct: A page must use ≥3 distinct recipes from the catalogue when `RHYTHM ≥ 4` (default). Pick the recipe whose intent matches each section's content shape; don't reach for the same recipe by default.

refusals.title: Pill wall — too many `<Pill>` per card or per section
refusals.rationale: Pills are status atoms. When a single card carries > 3 or a section carries > 8, pills lose their meaning and become page furniture. The 1.0 case-study page reached pill-wall density by section 4.
refusals.correct: Cap at 3 pills per card, 8 per section. If you need more, restructure into `<DotTimeline>`, `<ToolCall>` rows, or a `<DottedChart>` — the content shape is wrong, not the pill count.

refusals.title: Page uses fewer than 3 distinct composition recipes when RHYTHM ≥ 4
refusals.rationale: Without recipe variance, every page converges on the same shape. The 2.0 contract requires ≥3 distinct recipes per page at default `RHYTHM` to force layout variation.
refusals.correct: Choose ≥3 recipes whose intents match the page's content shapes. Default fallback set: MetricWall + RAGStatus + AgentLog covers most AI-product status surfaces.

refusals.title: MetricStat group without a shared unit or axis
refusals.rationale: A row of `<MetricStat>` reading `10`, `M2`, `5`, `2` mixes units that don't compose — count of stages, milestone label, count of fixtures, count of blockers. The numbers can't be compared. The 1.0 case-study page made this exact mistake twice.
refusals.correct: A `<MetricStat>` group of 2+ must share a unit OR a clear semantic axis named in the eyebrow (e.g., `EVIDENCE MATURITY — % PER STAGE`). If units don't compose, split into separate one-stat callouts or pick a single axis and exclude the outliers.

refusals.title: File path appears in body prose
refusals.rationale:
File paths in body prose (e.g., `consent_orders/docs/receipts/2026-04-28-smoke-matrix-expansion-receipt.md` set in a `

`) read like a status-meeting transcript, not interface copy. The 1.0 case-study page set three file paths inline in body prose.
refusals.correct: File paths belong in `<code>` blocks, `<ToolCall>` detail rows, footnotes, or `<Citation>` chips. Body prose names the artifact (`Three receipts. See the ReceiptStack below.`), the path renders elsewhere.

refusals.title: Adjacent sections share micro-template (database-dump rhythm)
refusals.rationale: When every card in a page ends with the same closer template (e.g., `Evidence: X. Next: Y.`), the page reads as a database dump rather than designed copy. Rhythm variance is required by the voice contract.
refusals.correct: Rotate among permitted card-closer templates: `Next: <subject-verb-object>.`, `Blocks on <subject>.`, `<Phase> remains <state>.`, `Owner: <name>.`, `Updates <event>.`, or no closer. Never two adjacent sections with the same closer.

refusals.title: Status-meeting phrasing in body prose, headlines, or eyebrows
refusals.pattern: (?:>|\b)(we are building|what we are building|what we're working on|things are stricter|main-branch truth|draft-PR truth|working-tree truth|still-blocked truth|where we are going|the project is not green because)\b
refusals.rationale: These phrasings come from engineering status meetings. They narrate the existence of the work rather than naming the state. The 1.0 case-study page used `we are building`, `things are stricter`, `main-branch truth`, and `where we are going` — every one of them obscured the actual state.
refusals.correct: Replace with the named substitute from the V7 banned-phrase catalogue. `We are building…` → name the artifact. `Where we are going` → `Five phases queued.` `Things are stricter` → name what got stricter and how. `Main-branch truth` (as adjective) → `Lands on main.`

refusals.title: Proposing or implementing a component for an existing family without running the family-extension protocol
refusals.rationale: New components in an existing family must share the family's visual grammar (primitive shape, state coloring, motion cadence, axis language, typography). Proposing options that break the grammar forces the user to pick between consistency and desired behavior — a false choice. The spec review passes, the component ships, and the family is now incoherent. The glance test — 'does it look like it belongs?' — is the actual test, and skipping the protocol guarantees failing it.
refusals.correct: Run the six-step 'Extending a component family' protocol in SKILL.md: (1) read every existing family member's source + preview, (2) state the motif in one sentence, (3) name the vocabulary (primitive, state colors, motion, axis, typography) explicitly, (4) propose only same-primitive variants (flag any breaking variant as a 'branching' conversation), (5) mock next to the canonical family member before implementation, (6) update section labels after shipping. Refuse to continue if any step is skipped.
appliesTo: any task phrased as 'add another X to the Y family'
any task asking for a new member of a named component family (Data Motif, Accent Carrier, Toast, etc.)
any task asking for a 'different version' or 'companion' to an existing component
